meaning: n.涨潮,变迁,[物]流量,通量vi.熔化,流出vt.使熔融,用焊剂处理
examples: Our society is in a state of flux.
Both quantum mechanics and chaos theory suggest a world constantly in flux.
Education remains in a state of flux which will take some time to settle down.
Though the recording industry remains in flux, Slim says he still finds confidence to keep going.
The gaming industry is in flux, but you are seeing points of clarity develop.
